notes: Pickleheads lists exactly 2 locations for San Leandro totaling 14 courts: Washington Manor Park (6 outdoor, free, city-operated) and Bay-O-Vista Swim and Tennis Club (8 outdoor, membership-required private club) -- consistent across the city index page and both venue detail pages, and cross-corroborated by half a dozen independent aggregators (Bounce, DailyPB, globalpickleball.network, whoplays.us, CourtsApp, CourtSource). The city's own official page, https://www.sanleandro.org/1640/Pickleball-Courts-and-Tennis-Courts, was located and used as the primary source per this site's standard -- it explicitly confirms Washington Manor Park has 6 pickleball courts (courts 1/3/5 reservable, 2/4/6 first-come-first-served) and explicitly shows Heath Park has tennis courts ONLY (4 tennis courts, 2 under renovation, no pickleball mentioned), so Heath Park was investigated and excluded. Bay-O-Vista does not appear on the city's page, which is expected and correctly so -- it is a private membership club, not a city facility; its existence and court count are instead corroborated by Pickleheads plus the club's own site (bovswim.com) plus three other independent aggregators. A third possible venue, Roosevelt Elementary School (951 Dowling Blvd), was flagged by exactly one aggregator (CourtSource.us, as a 'nearby' 2-court listing) claiming San Leandro has 3 pickleball venues / 16 courts total. This claim was investigated at length: it is NOT on Pickleheads (which stops at 2 locations), NOT on the city's own pickleball/tennis courts page, NOT on the school's own site, and NOT corroborated by any of the other 8+ aggregators checked (globalpickleball.network, Bounce, DailyPB, Pickleballify, HAQ Pickle, etc.), despite several of those directories clearly trying hard to enumerate every court in the city. San Leandro does have general joint-use agreements between the city and San Leandro Unified/San Lorenzo Unified school districts for after-hours access to some school hard courts, so it's plausible a school court exists in some form, but there is no confirmation it is striped/equipped for pickleball or open to the public without district contact. Given the single uncorroborated source and the fact it directly conflicts with the city's own enumeration, it was excluded from venues[] rather than included as a shaky record -- a human should call San Leandro Recreation (510-577-3462) or the school district directly to resolve this before adding it. Also considered and excluded: a single Swimply peer-to-peer backyard-court rental listing ($81/hr private residential court) -- not a stable public venue/club in the sense this directory tracks, and no other city page in this repo includes Swimply listings. Chabot College (a San Leandro institution) was checked and has no dedicated pickleball courts, only a PE course taught on existing courts. The Picklr (indoor chain) has no San Leandro location.

venues.name: Washington Manor Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court (one source specifies concrete), dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.reservable: Mixed -- Courts 1, 3, 5 reservable online through San Leandro Recreation; Courts 2, 4, 6 first-come, first-served
venues.hours: 8:00am-8:00pm daily (automatic lighting supports play until 8:00pm; operates on a 4-on/4-off rotation when players are waiting, per Pickleheads)
venues.weather: Mild bay-moderated climate typical of San Leandro's flats -- cool marine-layer mornings, warm dry summer afternoons, rare frost, and a steady afternoon breeze off the bay by evening.
venues.notes: Core facts (6 outdoor courts) are corroborated by the city's own official page AND Pickleheads AND four other independent aggregators -- the strongest-confirmed venue in this file. Two minor unresolved discrepancies for a human to note: (1) Address zip code -- the city's own facility detail page states 94577, while Pickleheads-derived listings and most third-party aggregators (dailypb, globalpickleball, haqpickle) state 94579 for the same 14900 Zelma St address; went with the city's own page as higher-authority. (2) Reservation model -- the official city page gives a granular breakdown (3 of 6 courts individually reservable online, 3 first-come-first-served), while Pickleheads' own copy simplifies this to a blanket 'cannot be reserved / free public play' framing; recorded the more detailed official version. One outlier, HAQ Pickle, states only 4 courts (vs. 6 everywhere else including the city itself) and lists the surface as 'unknown' -- treated as a low-confidence scraping error and not used. Pickleheads shows two distinct URL slugs for this same venue (san-leandro-pickleball-courts-washington-manor-park and washington-manor-park) that return identical facts; both listed under sources, the first used as the canonical pickleheadsUrl since it's what the city-index page itself links to. lat/lon sourced from a secondary park-mapping aggregator (mypacer.com), not the city directly or a confirmed Google pin -- flagged approxLocation. googleMapsUrl's numeric cid was derived by converting the hex feature-ID segment (0x621069b28a0bf5bb) from a Google Maps place URL found via search for this exact venue/address into decimal; not a place_id or /maps/place/ URL. Also has 2 separate tennis courts (not pickleball) per the official page, not counted here. Also note this facility is on the same 14900 Zelma St property as the San Leandro Family Aquatic Center and Kiddie Kollege building, which show up in some search noise but are unrelated to the pickleball courts.

venues.name: Bay-O-Vista Swim and Tennis Club
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets (per Pickleheads); see notes for a conflicting lower-confidence source
venues.netType: Dedicated, permanent nets (per Pickleheads); see notes for a conflicting lower-confidence source
venues.price: Membership required (specific dues not publicly listed)
venues.reservable: Not reservable -- members/guests only, walk-up within membership access
venues.hours: Club hours (not pickleball-specific): Mon-Fri 5:30am-9:30pm, Sat-Sun 6:00am-9:00pm, per the club's own site
venues.weather: Sits up in San Leandro's eastern hills near Lake Chabot -- noticeably cooler and breezier than the shoreline flats, with less fog but more direct exposure to afternoon wind.
venues.notes: Existence and core facts (8 outdoor courts) are corroborated by Pickleheads, whoplays.us, and the club's own site (bovswim.com references 'eight courts' in a photo caption) -- three independent sources agreeing, including the primary venue itself. Not on the city's own recreation page, which is expected and correct since this is a private club, not a city facility. Several secondary-field conflicts recorded rather than resolved: (1) Pickleballify is a clear outlier claiming only 4 courts, a one-time fee (vs. membership), and bring-your-own portable nets (vs. dedicated permanent nets) -- internally inconsistent with its own page header ('1 Pickleball Court in San Leandro') and contradicted by every other source, so not used, but flagged for a human to weigh. (2) Lights -- Pickleheads-derived listings state lights are available as an amenity, while CourtSource explicitly and specifically states 'no lighted courts'; this is a direct contradiction between two otherwise-reasonable sources, so the 'lights' field was left 'Not specified' rather than picking a side. (3) Hours -- the club's own site states 5:30am/6am opening times, while CourtSource lists a very different, much later schedule (10am or 11:30am opening depending on day); used the club's own site as higher-authority but flagging the discrepancy since it could reflect pickleball-specific vs. general club/pool hours. No exact coordinates or Google Maps cid were found from any source checked; left null rather than guessing given zero data points (unlike Washington Manor Park, where at least one secondary aggregator supplied a specific lat/lon).
